title: AIM Persistent-State Schema (aim-persistent-state-v1.0)
description: Schema for the AGM monitor's `persistent-state.json` — the canonical knowledge-base file the Applier writes to (G1+G2, AD-2026-04-26Y). The MVP shape is intentionally permissive on per-module structure (`additionalProperties: true` everywhere outside `_meta`); per-module strictness is the responsibility of the MBC sweeps that own each module's contract. The strict layer is the `_meta` block — `schema_version`, `last_updated`, `last_issue`, `monitor_slug`. The Applier engine bumps `_meta.last_updated` and `_meta.last_issue` on any cycle that lands at least one patch (Q5 ratification 2026-04-26 — null cycles are no-ops). Sprint-5 Phase 2 (BRIEF cf14eabd, E10 cure) adds a top-level `arrays` block declaring, for every persistent-state array AGM cares about, the merge `key_field` and a `key_constraint` (regex pattern OR string enum). The engine (`pipeline.engine.persistent_state.apply_patches_to_persistent_state`, amended in Phase 1) reads these declarations to enforce additive merge semantics and raise `DisjointKeySchemeError` on key-scheme rotation.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this file. New canonical value: `aim-persistent-state-v1.0`. Legacy bake value `2.0` (string or number) is accepted during the AD-2026-04-26Y bake window. A follow-up AD will retire the legacy values once MBC sweeps stamp every monitor's persistent-state.json with the canonical version.

last_updated.description: Most recent material state change. Either ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD, what the Applier writes per Q5 ratification) or full ISO-8601 timestamp (legacy MBC-stamped). Bumped by the Applier on cycles that land at least one patch; NOT bumped on null cycles.

last_issue.description: Issue counter. The Applier increments this by 1 on cycles that land at least one patch when the current value is an integer; left untouched otherwise (string-based versioning is per-module, not engine-stamped in MVP).

module_2.models.describe: Per-model AGM tracking entries (module_2). `name` is the canonical model identifier (e.g. 'GPT-5.1', 'Claude Opus 4.7'). Free-form pattern — lab naming conventions are not stable enough to enum-lock.

module_7_risk_vectors.describe: AGM Risk Vectors register (module_7) — closed taxonomy of 18 vectors carried in current persistent-state. Enum-locked to prevent the LLM from renaming vectors mid-cycle (the E10 pathology). New vectors require an explicit schema bump.

module_9_eu_ai_act_tracker.layers.describe: EU AI Act 7-layer architecture tracker. `layer` is an integer 1..7; stored as int but matched by stringified value at engine merge time.

module_14_concentration_index.domains.describe: Concentration-index domain rows (module_14). Free-form domain names (Models, Talent, Regulatory Access, etc.) — accept any non-empty string.

module_15_aisi_pipeline.watch_list.describe: AISI / government AI body watch list (module_15). `institution` is the canonical organisation name.

ongoing_lab_postures.describe: Per-lab posture tracking. `actor` is the lab/organisation name (e.g. 'Anthropic', 'OpenAI', 'SpaceX/xAI'). Free-form pattern — labs come and go via mergers; enum-locking would force a schema bump on every market event.

cross_monitor_flags.flags.describe: Cross-monitor flag identifiers. Pattern lock matches the cmf-NNN scheme in live state. The shared cross-monitor flag registry uses this id-shape contract — prevents the LLM from rotating to CMF-001 / cmf001 / flag-001 variants.

gpai_compliance.labs.describe: GPAI Code-of-Practice signatory compliance tracker. `lab` is the lab name (overlaps with ongoing_lab_postures but is the GPAI-specific row).

country_grid_status.jurisdictions.describe: Per-jurisdiction AI-law status grid. `jurisdiction` is currently free-form (EU, USA, UK, China, India, Canada, Brazil...); ISO-3166-1 alpha-2 normalisation deferred (Phase 3 ESA precedent may motivate a later bump).

calibration_log.describe: Append-only calibration log (one entry per material methodology change). `date` is ISO date or full ISO-8601 timestamp — the engine treats this array as additive-only by construction.

gaps_register.describe: Open-findings register (gaps the monitor has identified but not yet resolved). Pattern lock matches the AGM-GAP-NNN scheme — prevents Interpreter from rotating to free-form gap_ids.

jurisdiction_risk_matrix.describe: AGM per-jurisdiction risk matrix declared in the interpreter-schema. Currently an interpreter-output transient (not yet promoted to persistent-state at the top level), but forward-declared here so the Phase 2 coverage gate exits clean. If/when promoted, engine merge semantics apply automatically.

key_judgments.describe: AGM key-judgments array declared in the interpreter-schema. Currently an interpreter-output transient (not yet promoted to persistent-state at the top level), but forward-declared here so the Phase 2 coverage gate exits clean. Pattern lock against AGM-KJ-NNN scheme.
